title: Gentle Flaky British Wholemeal Flour Victoria Sponge
description: A traditional British dessert with a twist, featuring wholemeal flour and a flaky texture, cooked to perfection in a clay pot, this plant-based friendly Victoria sponge is a must try.

ingredients.name: plant-based milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond or soy milk

instructions: Preheat the clay pot in a cool oven at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 10 minutes to prevent cracking.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the wholemeal flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the plant-based milk, canola oil, apple cider vinegar, and maple syrup.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Add the vanilla extract and stir gently to distribute.
Pour the batter into the preheated clay pot and smooth the top.
Bake for 25 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 275 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove the sponge from the oven and let it cool in the pot for 5 minutes before transferring it to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once the sponge is completely cool, use a serrated knife to level it, if necessary, and split it in half horizontally using a long serrated knife.
Dust the top of the sponge with powdered sugar, if desired, and serve.
